text: The Latin here mentions four destructive agencies—the caterpillar, two kinds of locust, and the blight. The Hebrew text is ordinarily understood as giving four different kinds of locust, or four different names for the locust. It is impossible to say with certainty whether the prophet, from 1.4 to 2.11, is describing a hostile invasion under the metaphor of a locust-swarm, or a locust-swarm under the metaphor of a hostile invasion.
